specification.topic-statement: The fuzzy filter scores and ranks a candidate string list against a search query, returning the best-matching results up to a caller-specified limit.

scope.in-scope: Prefix filtering, fuzzy subsequence scoring, result sorting, and result count limiting.
scope.boundary: The fuzzy filter is invoked by the tab-completion system to rank completion candidates. Candidate generation and selection are handled externally.
Subsequence matching and scoring are delegated to the vendored fzy library. The fzy library's behaviour is not specified here.

field.name: search
field.note: may be empty; must not be null

field.name: candidate
field.note: points into the original candidates array; not a copy

field.name: result array
field.note: talloc-owned by the caller's context; null when no results

field.name: count
field.note: number of entries in the result array

behavior.prefix-filter:
Before fuzzy scoring is applied, candidates are filtered by prefix. A candidate passes only if it begins with the search string, compared case-insensitively. Candidates that do not pass the prefix test are excluded from scoring entirely.
An empty search string passes all candidates through the prefix filter, because every string begins with an empty prefix.
behavior.fuzzy-scoring: Each candidate that passed the prefix filter is tested for a fuzzy match: the search string's characters must all appear in the candidate in order (not necessarily consecutively). Candidates that fail this test are excluded. Candidates that pass receive a numeric score; higher scores indicate a closer match.
behavior.sorting: Scored candidates are sorted in descending score order. Candidates with higher scores appear first in the result array.
behavior.result-limiting:
If the number of matching candidates exceeds max_results, only the first max_results entries (the highest-scoring) are returned. If the number of matches is at or below max_results, all matches are returned.
If no candidates match, null is returned and the output count is zero.
behavior.notable: Result entries hold pointers directly into the caller-supplied candidates array. The caller must ensure candidate strings remain valid for as long as the result array is in use.
When candidate_count is zero, the function returns null immediately without consulting the search string.
